workflow: RupeeKit Workflow 1 - Keyword Research and Content Plan

plan.searchIntent: Help salaried employees calculate possible HRA exemption using salary, rent, city type, and HRA received.
plan.targetPersona: Salaried employees in India seeking to optimize their tax savings through HRA exemption and understand HRA rules.
plan.primaryGoal: To provide an accurate and easy-to-use HRA exemption calculator for Indian salaried employees, helping them understand their potential tax savings and improve organic search visibility for HRA-related queries.
titleOptions: HRA Exemption Calculator India - Calculate Your Tax Savings
Online HRA Calculator India | Maximize Your Tax Exemption
Calculate HRA Exemption in India - RupeeKit Tool
plan.recommendedMetaTitle: HRA Exemption Calculator India - Calculate Your Tax Savings
plan.recommendedMetaDescription: Calculate HRA exemption in India using salary, HRA received, rent paid, and metro or non-metro city rules.

inputs.name: basicSalary
inputs.label: Basic Salary (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter your monthly basic salary component.

inputs.name: dearnessAllowance
inputs.label: Dearness Allowance (DA) (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter your monthly Dearness Allowance, if applicable and part of retirement benefits.

inputs.name: commission
inputs.label: Commission (if fixed % of turnover) (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter monthly commission if it's a fixed percentage of turnover.

inputs.name: hraReceived
inputs.label: HRA Received (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter the actual HRA amount you receive from your employer monthly.

inputs.name: actualRentPaid
inputs.label: Actual Rent Paid (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter the actual monthly rent you pay for your accommodation.

inputs.name: cityType
inputs.label: City Type
inputs.helpText: Select if you reside in a Metro city (Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai) or Non-Metro city.

calculatorSpec.formulaPlainEnglish: The HRA exemption is the least of the following three amounts: 1. Actual HRA received from your employer. 2. 50% of (Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance + Commission) if you live in a metro city, or 40% for non-metro cities. 3. Actual rent paid minus 10% of (Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance + Commission).
calculatorSpec.formula: MIN(HRA_Received, (City_Type == 'metro' ? 0.50 : 0.40) * (Basic_Salary + DA + Commission), Actual_Rent_Paid - (0.10 * (Basic_Salary + DA + Commission)))

contentBrief.introAngle: Start by highlighting how HRA exemption can significantly reduce taxable income for salaried individuals in India, and introduce the calculator as a quick, reliable tool to determine their eligible exemption.
mustCover: What is HRA and its purpose?
Eligibility criteria for claiming HRA exemption (salaried, rent payment, no self-owned property)
Detailed explanation of the three conditions for HRA exemption calculation.
Definition of 'salary' for HRA purposes (Basic + DA + Commission).
Difference between Metro and Non-Metro cities for HRA calculation.
Documents required to claim HRA (rent receipts, landlord's PAN if rent > ₹1 Lakh/year).
How to claim HRA exemption in ITR.
Impact of HRA on overall tax liability.
Scenarios where HRA exemption might not be applicable (e.g., living with parents, not paying rent).
contentBrief.exampleScenario: Priya lives in Bengaluru (Non-Metro). Her monthly Basic Salary is ₹60,000, HRA received is ₹25,000, and she pays ₹18,000 in rent. Calculate her HRA exemption.
faqQuestions: Can I claim HRA if I live with my parents?
What if my landlord doesn't have a PAN card?
Is HRA exemption available if I own a house but live in a rented one?
How is 'salary' defined for HRA calculation?
What are the metro cities for HRA purposes?
Do I need to submit rent receipts to my employer?
trustNotes: Uses commonly followed Indian HRA exemption calculation rules for educational purposes.
Explains assumptions clearly so users can understand how the estimate is calculated.
Designed as an informational calculator, not as personalized tax or financial advice.
Users should verify final tax treatment with official guidance or a qualified tax professional.
contentBrief.disclaimer: This calculator is for educational and informational purposes only. It provides an estimate based on the inputs and assumptions shown. It is not tax, legal, investment, or financial advice. Please verify with official sources or consult a qualified professional before making tax or financial decisions.
